But it seems to work as a proof of concept :)

author: @kreynolds1123

I was searching tesla turbine compressor to see if there was anything on this, so as to get an idea how well it would work or not. My inclination is that its capability to compress is in part, a function of disk diameter and rate of rotation but how does it compare to a centrifugal compressor in efficiency?

author: @DeliciousDeBlair
text:
IDEALLY if you can make the fan/pump run from the gas generated in the combustion chamber!

"Jut exactly HOW would you do that?" somebunny may ask...

Why SIMPLE!

The same way you would add boost to ANY NORMALLY fueled engine!

You have roots blowers, Whipple superchargers and exhaust gas driven turbochargers!

ALL of these work PERFECTLY FINE off the output of the engine in such a manner as to EASILY and QUITE EFFICIENTLY draw the air-fuel mixture out from the wood gassifier to begin with! 

       /)/) 
    (  'ˬ') 
@(>_ )-b 
     \) \)

author: @xristidi
text:
I'm afraid that the inner workings of the turbine will get clogged very fast. The gasifier's smoke is very rich in suspainded particulated matter , which is also quite sticky... u need a system that draws the air "indirectly" or through a secondary action that does not expose any moving parts to the gasses being produced... 
great work as always!!

I found that warmed and hot air didn't work very well.

author: @MrAnderson4509
text: Seems like the Tesla turbine would fit the bill and that it provides brush/spark ignition separation from the gas, and may be isolated from the heat, and the fact that you have it, And you have completed the build, it just seems to fit, nice job, and congratulations on mastering that brazing rod👍There is a soldering iron I saw in a very early popular magazine called "science and mechanics" where was highlighted a man who used a carbon rod and Lionell train power supply speed control transformer (variac ?)  and was able to braze aluminum together along with many other things by making direct contact with the carbon Rod and heating the metal, there was also an explanation of a few fluxes or a surface preparation he used to achieve all this braising/soldering action, I believe the magazines are still accessible in a pdf archive, it was a Cover highlight, again thank you for all your videos, they are quite splendid.👍 still have not figured out how to become a supporter, as simple or obvious as this may be, a bit of personal instruction on that would help me a great deal if you have ever time. ty

author: @TerryGilsenan
text: I suspect you may need a way to bypass that once the motor starts because that is unlikely to have enough flow-through capacity to allow the motor to continue running. Also, it seemed to be running the wrong way for the housing...?

replies.author: @nil5221
replies.text: Yes for this type of design, the blower (sucker?) is run only to start the burn. Once the motor fires up, it draws the flow through and the blower is switched off. Hence Robert says "it only needs to run 3-4 minutes to start the gasifier"

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yes mate - that's the plan
